name: Music Assistant - Create Playlist (MCP)

nodes.name: HA - Search Track
nodes.notes: Replace HA_CREDENTIAL_ID with your actual credential ID. Create HTTP Header Auth credential with: Name=Authorization, Value=Bearer YOUR_HA_TOKEN

notes:
Music Assistant Playlist Creator (via Home Assistant Services)

## Setup Required
1. Create HTTP Header Auth credential in n8n:
   - Name: Home Assistant Token
   - Header Name: Authorization
   - Header Value: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiI5NjUzNmFkMmQ1Yzg0ZjA4YjgyMWM3OGM0MDE4N2ZkZCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MzI2NTg4OCwiZXhwIjoyMDg4NjI1ODg4fQ.0ys5aUxTjk3XXSSVjADWyLzShMj2W0lGzQKmAfV3k6I

2. Update the credential references in 'HA - Search Track' and 'HA - Play/Queue Track' nodes

## Endpoint
POST http://192.168.5.138:5678/webhook/mcp/music-assistant/create-playlist

## Request Body
{
  "title": "Playlist Name",
  "songs": ["Artist - Track", "Artist - Track"],
  "player_id": "media_player.office_2",
  "enqueue": "play"  // 'play' (replace queue), 'add' (append), 'next' (play next)
}

## IMPORTANT: Player ID
Use the _2 suffix players (e.g., media_player.office_2, media_player.kitchen_nest_2)
These are the Music Assistant versions. The base players (without _2) are Cast devices
and won't work with music_assistant.play_media.

## Response
{
  "success": true,
  "title": "Playlist Name",
  "playerId": "media_player.office_2",
  "addedCount": 3,
  "missingCount": 0,
  "added": [{"song": "...", "trackUri": "..."}],
  "missing": []
}

## Notes
- This creates a queue on the specified player, not a persistent playlist
- First track uses the specified enqueue mode, subsequent tracks use 'add'
- config_entry_id: 01KGKQVQ8BYXXKMXE3DCZZEZ87
